title: Workflow Run
description: Canonical state schema for a durable workflow run. A workflow run is the top-level unit of governed agent execution in ao-kernel: it threads an intent (issue / spec / prompt) through a sequence of steps (context compile, adapter invocation, diff preview, CI gate, human approval, patch apply, PR open, evidence replay), with CAS-backed revision tokens, durable checkpoints, fail-closed budget, and JSONL evidence events. PR-A0 defines the shape; state machine implementation lands in Tranche A PR-A1. Referential integrity with agent-adapter-contract (adapter_refs) and policy files (policy_refs) is narrative at PR-A0; loader-level validation lands in Tranche A PR-A2.

run_id.description: Unique workflow run identifier (UUIDv4). Referenced by agent-adapter-contract.input_envelope.run_id and all evidence paths under '.ao/evidence/workflows/{run_id}/'.

workflow_id.description: Workflow registry identifier (e.g., 'bug_fix_flow', 'review_ai_flow'). snake_case, 2-64 chars. Registry is populated at Tranche A PR-A2.

workflow_version.description: Registered workflow version in SemVer 2.0. Pins the workflow definition revision at run start; migrations across versions are handled at workflow registry level.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the run was registered in the canonical store.

started_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when state transitioned from 'created' to 'running'. Absent until first transition.

updated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent state mutation. Updated on every CAS write.

completed_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when state transitioned to a terminal value (completed / failed / cancelled). Absent for non-terminal runs.

revision.description: CAS revision token: 64-char SHA-256 hex digest of the canonical run record. Callers treat as opaque (ao_kernel/context/agent_coordination.py::get_revision convention; canonical_store._mutate_with_cas write path).

intent.description: Input specification. One of: issue URL, markdown file path, or inline prompt.

steps.description: Sequential step records. Append-only: steps append as the run progresses. Mutation of a completed step is forbidden (enforced by CAS).

checkpoint.description: Most recent durable checkpoint. Absent until first step completes. Compatible with ao_kernel/_internal/session/context_store and roadmap/checkpoint patterns.

policy_refs.description: Workspace-relative policy files referenced at run start. Must include 'ao_kernel/defaults/policies/policy_worktree_profile.v1.json' when any adapter invocation is expected. Other policies: policy_tool_calling.v1.json, policy_mcp_memory.v1.json, policy_secrets.v1.json.

adapter_refs.description: adapter_id values used in this run. Narrative cross-reference to agent-adapter-contract.schema.v1.json::adapter_id (value match, not JSON Schema $ref). Registry validation at Tranche A PR-A2.

evidence_refs.description: JSONL paths where evidence events for this run are emitted. Standard patterns: '.ao/evidence/workflows/{run_id}/events.jsonl' (workflow timeline), '.ao/evidence/workflows/{run_id}/adapter-{adapter_id}.jsonl' (adapter logs), '.ao/evidence/workflows/{run_id}/manifest.json' (SHA-256 integrity manifest, workspace artefact convention per CLAUDE.md §2).

budget.description: Run-level budget. Aggregated over all adapter invocations. fail_closed_on_exhaust enforced.

approvals.description: Governance approval events (HITL gate on CI/diff/PR). Separate domain from adapter interrupt_request ($defs/interrupt_request): approvals are orchestrator-mediated, interrupts are adapter-mediated.

error.description: Populated only when state=failed. Mirrors agent-adapter-contract.output_envelope.error structure for category consistency.

state_enum.description: Workflow run lifecycle state. created: record exists, not started. running: adapter or step in progress. interrupted: agent requested HITL via adapter interrupt_token (resume via adapter). waiting_approval: governance gate requires human approval (resume via approval_token). applying: diff is being applied to worktree. verifying: CI / test gate is running. completed / failed / cancelled: terminal. Allowed transitions (narrative; enforced at Tranche A PR-A1 impl): created -> [running, cancelled]; running -> [interrupted, waiting_approval, applying, failed, cancelled]; interrupted -> [running, failed, cancelled]; waiting_approval -> [applying, failed, cancelled]; applying -> [verifying, failed, cancelled]; verifying -> [completed, failed, cancelled]; completed/failed/cancelled -> (terminal). Note on distinction: 'interrupted' = agent-initiated (adapter output_envelope.status=interrupted, interrupt_token); 'waiting_approval' = governance-initiated (policy_refs trigger HITL gate, approval_token). Two separate token domains; see $defs/approval and $defs/interrupt_request.

kind.description: Intent shape discriminator. issue_url: GitHub/GitLab issue URL. markdown_file: path to spec / issue body markdown. inline_prompt: raw prompt text. spec_ref: reference to a registered spec document.

payload.description: Intent content: URL, path, raw text, or spec ID. Interpreted according to 'kind'.

metadata.description: Optional. Labels, priority, source system identifiers.

step_id.description: Unique step identifier within the run. Stable across replay.

state.description: Per-step state. Mirrors run-level state enum plus 'skipped' (for conditional-skipped steps in branching flows, FAZ-D native #9 branching).

actor.description: Who executed this step. adapter: external agent invocation. ao-kernel: internal orchestrator (context compile, diff apply, PR open). human: HITL approval / interrupt response. system: automated infrastructure (CI gate result, timeout).

input_ref.description: Pointer to step input (e.g., a context pack path, an adapter input_envelope serialization).

output_ref.description: Pointer to step output (e.g., adapter output_envelope serialization, CI result JSON, patch artifact path). PR-A4 requires this field to be populated for actor in {adapter, ao-kernel, system} steps that produce durable outputs (diff content, reverse-diff paths, CI result JSON) so crash-recovery can find the canonical artifact.

capability_output_refs.description: Map<capability, run-relative artifact path>. Per-capability typed artifacts written by MultiStepDriver from InvocationResult.extracted_outputs (walker output_parse rules). Absent for pre-B6 runs and for adapters without output_parse manifest rules. PR-B6 additive widen; plan v4 §2.2 driver-owned materialization.

attempt.description: Step execution attempt number. 1 for first attempt; 2 when step.on_failure=retry_once triggers a re-execution. Append-only semantics: a retry produces a NEW step_record with the same step_name, a fresh step_id, and attempt=2. Only the terminal (highest attempt) step_record for a given step_name is authoritative for run-level state transitions. Crash-safety: if an attempt=2 step_record is absent and the prior attempt=1 is failed with on_failure=retry_once, the driver MUST resume by creating attempt=2 (not treat retry as consumed). Introduced in PR-A4.

evidence_event_ids.description: ULID event_ids emitted during this step. Cross-reference to events.jsonl for replay.

adapter_id.description: Adapter invoked in this step (when actor=adapter). Cross-reference to agent-adapter-contract.

checkpoint_id.description: Unique checkpoint identifier within the run.

sha256.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the canonical run record at checkpoint time. Matches workspace integrity manifest per CLAUDE.md §2.

jsonl_pointer.description: Workspace-relative path to the JSONL progress log at checkpoint time (e.g., '.ao/runs/{run_id}/checkpoint-{n}.jsonl').

step_completed_up_to.description: step_id of the most recently completed step at checkpoint time. Resume from the next step.

tokens.description: LLM token ceiling, aggregated across all adapter invocations in this run. When tokens_input + tokens_output are also configured (PR-B2), aggregate equals sum of granular axes.

tokens_input.description: Prompt (input) token ceiling — granular breakdown of tokens aggregate (PR-B2 additive widen). Writer always emits when configured; reader populates from legacy 'tokens' axis when absent.

tokens_output.description: Completion (output) token ceiling — granular breakdown of tokens aggregate (PR-B2 additive widen). Writer omits when not configured (null serialization is invalid).

time_seconds.description: Wall-clock budget ceiling (seconds). Applies to the whole run including HITL wait time (so long-running approvals count).

cost_usd.description: USD cost ceiling, aggregated across adapter invocations using the provider price catalog (FAZ-B extension).

fail_closed_on_exhaust.description: MUST be true. When any budget axis reaches its limit, state transitions to failed with error.category=budget_exhausted. CNS-007 fail-closed pattern; TRANCHE-STRATEGY-V2.md §3 hard budget cap.

approval_id.description: Unique approval identifier within the run.

approval_token.description: Opaque resume token handed to the approver. Distinct from adapter interrupt_token ($defs/interrupt_request.interrupt_token) — separate domain. Presented to ao-kernel on resume to transition state from waiting_approval to applying (granted) or cancelled (denied).

gate.description: Which orchestrator gate triggered this approval. pre_diff: before showing adapter's diff preview. pre_apply: before applying diff to worktree. pre_pr: before opening PR. pre_merge: before merging PR (if ao-kernel orchestrates merge). post_ci: after CI result surfaces. custom: policy-defined gate.

decision.description: Outcome. granted: proceed. denied: run transitions to cancelled. timeout: no response within gate deadline (treated as denied by fail-closed default).

actor.description: Human identifier (GitHub login, email, or internal actor ID) of the approver.

payload.description: Gate-specific context: diff summary, CI report pointer, risk flags. Subject to policy_worktree_profile.evidence_redaction.

interrupt_id.description: Unique interrupt identifier within the run.

interrupt_token.description: Opaque resume token minted by the adapter. Distinct from governance approval_token ($defs/approval.approval_token) — separate domain. Presented on adapter resume to continue the paused invocation.

question_payload.description: Adapter-defined question shape. Subject to redaction.

response_payload.description: Human-supplied response shape. Populated only after resumed_at. Subject to redaction.

message.description: Human-readable error message. Subject to evidence_redaction.

category.description: Failure category. Extends agent-adapter-contract error.category with run-level categories: approval_denied (governance gate refused), ci_failed (test/lint gate blocked), apply_conflict (diff could not be cleanly applied).
